prompt: Create an ultra-wide 3:1 cinematic product photograph of a small white matchbox lying diagonally on a smooth gray concrete floor in hard late-afternoon sunlight. The matchbox is partly open on the right, revealing exactly 5 wooden matches with pale sticks and dark tips, neatly aligned inside the tray. The top of the box has minimalist black lowercase typography reading maison de l'architecture et de la ville, spaced across the lid in a sparse modern layout, with a thin muted red-orange striker strip visible along the lower side edge. Strong architectural shadows fall across the scene, including one large dark rectangular shadow stretching from the upper right and another softer diagonal shadow passing behind the box. Add a delicate curved rainbow-like caustic light flare arcing above and around the matchbox, with subtle shimmering light patterns on the concrete near the lower left, as if sunlight is refracting through glass. Keep the composition minimal, quiet, and editorial, with lots of negative space, realistic texture, soft film grain, high dynamic range, natural color, sharp focus on the matchbox, and a refined contemporary architecture-magazine aesthetic.
